section_title: INTRODUCTION TO HIGHER TIME FRAME CONCEPTS AND KEY LEVELS
content:
This teaching is specifically dealing with higher timeframe concepts.
The points in focus for this module will be talking about key levels and what makes a key level.
Key Levels are defined as:
- Yearly highs and lows: This means the last 12 months, using a rolling calendar (not a specific January to
December phenomenon). Whatever today's date is, go back 12 months and identify the yearly high and low
respective to those points.
- Monthly highs and lows: Look at the last three months' high and low, and also just the last 30 days or the
previous month's high and low.
- Weekly highs and lows: Look back at last week's high and low, very simply.
What we are looking for is the long term macro yearly highs and lows, to quarterly, monthly highs and lows,
and the individual monthly highs and lows, and the weekly highs and lows. The yearly definition again is a
rolling calendar from whatever today is, go back 12 months, whatever the highest high and the lowest low
is, we'll use that as a reference point.

Price rejections are a critical concept:
- Double-top sweeps: This means when we see price action creating very clean equal highs or relatively
equal highs, we're expecting that phenomenon to take place for price to drive above those equal highs,
reject, and trade lower.
- Double-bottom sweeps: This is the reverse. Anytime in the price action we see equal lows form, we would
anticipate should price trade back down to that level, close to it, it would sweep below it, run the sell stops
below the equal lows, and then reject and go higher.
- Simple run on old lows: This means running out a previous low and then rejecting that and going higher.
- Runs on old highs: This is the obvious reverse. An old high gets traded through, a false breakout, and
then returns back into the range trading the opposite direction.

section_title: HIGHER TIME FRAME ANALYSIS FOR LIMITED INTRADAY TRADERS
content:
We will go through the majors tonight to discuss concepts new traders should focus on, to formulate an
idea or understanding to build a foundation. This teaching is really aimed at individuals who cannot do a lot
of intraday charting, trading, day trading, or scalping.
For an individual with this limitation, what is available in terms of analysis or techniques that we can more
or less ferret out an opportunity to practice with our demo account?

- IDENTIFYING TREND AND LIQUIDITY
First, I like to cycle through all the majors, but I begin with the Dollar Index (DXY). From a DXY daily chart,
we can see that we have been trading lower ever since around August, then consolidating and going
sideways. The prevailing trend has been down for the entire year on the Dollar. We created a rather
significant low here and have rallied up, breaking this high. Since this happened, we've been consolidating
sideways. Also, notice that we have traded below this low and have been inching higher.
Using the things mentioned at the beginning of the video, we look for relatively obvious support and
resistance levels:
- Long-term yearly highs and lows
- Monthly highs and lows
- Quarterly highs and lows
- Previous weeks' highs and lows
I won't draw all these levels on the chart as it could make a four-hour video and it's not essential; you can
go through charts and do that. The idea is first and foremost to see where we are trading right now. Are we
in a trending model, or are we in a trading range market? Right now, we are in a range-bound market or it
could potentially be a reversal. If we look at the trend, it's been lower, we have a potential reversal point
down here, and we've gone sideways. What could eventually happen is we could shoot higher and aim for
these equal highs, or we could roll right over and make an attempt to go lower and retest this low or try to
trade down to it and fail. You don't need to know that. The question I often get is, 'How do I know if it's not
going to go below this low with it wasn't stopped or if it goes to it and continues?' You have to figure out
where the market is right now. If you believe it's going to go down there, that offers up a range; that's your
opportunity and range of potential profit.
As it is here, I like to look at what was the most recent run on liquidity. Most recently, we've had it trade
above this high, sold off a little bit, and then came right back roaring higher trying to maybe run through
these equal highs again. If we do that, we may be reaching for these equal highs. Notice also this low was
traded through with this low, so we've already tried to go lower below this low and it rejected it handsomely.
We tried to go above this high here, tried to sell off, but quickly came back. This could still roll over and go
lower, but right now we're at a crossroads where if it goes any higher from here and we trade up above
these equal highs, that may warrant a run to these equal highs, and if it does that, that may be a precursor
to a run even higher.

section_title: SEASONAL TENDENCIES AND FRACTAL PRICE ACTION
content:
Because of the time of the year (end of 2017, late December), the market could just go sideways between
the high and the low defined here and just stay at equilibrium. Things you'll learn with experience is there
are certain times of the year when the market is predisposed to do certain things. Certain times of the year
it's supposed to trend, and certain times of the year it goes into consolidation. Right now, typically, we're in
a consolidation time. If you look back at previous years, we have the market trending prior to it and then
going into consolidation. Then around March, we start trending down to around August or September, then
it goes into some consolidation again. So, the idea of profiling is beneficial.
The main thing to take away from this presentation is that even though you're not looking at lower
timeframes, not scalping or day trading, those same price action concepts still apply because price is
fractal. That means the things we would see and expect to see unfold in the lower timeframes, just not tied
to any real time element, we would still expect the same phenomenon to occur on higher timeframe charts
like the daily.

- CONTRASTING DXY
If we go over to the Euro Dollar, you can see the opposite has happened. This market has trended higher
as the Dollar Index itself traded lower. We've seen the market trade sideways, as we saw on the Dollar
Index. Let's take a look at some ideas here:
- We've had a high traded through it.
- It broke down, took out that swing low, traded back up and broke it.
- Now we broke this high, tried to go higher, and now we're sitting with equal lows.
- We have these lows taken with this low.
Again, same scenario: we could be stuck in this range for the remainder of December. If I were forced to
take a trade where we're at right now, I would expect it to trade lower. Will it run through these relative
equal lows? It looks a little bit cleaner for that to occur in the opposite direction with the Dollar Index
because it has equal highs up here, and the market could in fact try to trade above these equal highs and
reach for that on the Euro.
We have a slightly lower low comparable to this low, but nonetheless, we could be overall retracing. Why
would that be most likely to occur and not higher? Because we've had the market move one direction all
year long, then we went into consolidation, and we broke above this high here, which should have run
higher, but now we keep breaking a low. So, any further movement below these equal lows, I would see
that as momentum building to make an attempt to get below these lows here. How much further below that,
we'd have to weigh that out and measure it at the time. But for now, I would anticipate, because of the time
of year (late December), the market's already moved, we're kind of 'long in the tooth' for that move, it would
be reasonable or healthy to expect some measure of retracement. Now, if it trades above this high, then we
would be aiming for these highs to be taken out and probably a measured move, something like this low to
this high, add that range to this low, and you would get some kind of a projection. But right now, I'm thinking
neutral to bearish on the Eurodollar.

- UNCERTAINTY AND LIQUIDITY RUNS
Cable has had a nice uptrending market here, and this one had an ability to get above and outside of this
range. We traded up into almost this swing high being taken out. But this one's actually a very difficult
market to be in right now; it's not clear, it's not obvious as to what it would want to do. So, even though
we've had a nice move earlier this year, we've gone into a condition where it's highly suspect in terms of
what would be the most likely or probable direction it's going to move next.
This looks like a classic chart pattern where folks would probably see that as maybe a bull flag and maybe
continuation to the upside, but thinking about what I showed you in the Dollar Index, that kind of negates
this. It doesn't mean it can't spike up there and do whatever it wants to do and run against the scenario with
the Dollar Index. For now, I just think we may end up seeing this thing go sideways still, or try to make an
attempt to go back below these equal lows here because we've already cleared out the equal highs, spent
some time up here, no follow-through yet. But if we trade down and break the middle of this range defined
by this high to this low, if we go back below that (easiest way to do if we get below this swing low), I think
that would be fair enough. If we break that, we would say price tried to make an attempt to get down below
here to get these double bottoms. So, we would expect a double bottom sweep only because we've already
seen a double top sweep here.
We're in uncertain conditions for Cable. Why? Because the dollar looks like it wants to go higher. And even
though Cable can go higher in that environment because of some fundamental factor that I don't know
about, it could happen, and that's the risk we have in trading, that's why we have to have stop losses and
such. But I don't see that that's likely to occur given the conditions in the marketplace here. So, this pair I
wouldn't touch. But if I had a gun to my head, I would be reaching for these equal lows because of the
Dollar bullishness as outlined in the video.

section_title: AUSSIE DOLLAR (AUD/USD) AND NEW ZEALAND DOLLAR (NZD/USD) DAILY CHART
content:
ANALYSIS
- SIDELINES DUE TO LACK OF CLARITY
Aussie Dollar (AUD/USD): This market has gone back into the middle of the range; it doesn't have any real
clear direction. We've had a move up, retracement, move up, and now we retraced; we're essentially in the
middle of the range. So, in terms of trading, this would probably be a good day trading pair, but for short
- term trading or swing trading, I wouldn't touch this one. Why? Because it doesn't have a defined movement,
and it's made an attempt to get quickly back to equilibrium or the middle of the range. The range is defined
by this low to this high, and we're smack dab right in the middle of it right now. We tried to go lower, but this
run here, we've outlined this in a previous video with SMT (Smart Money Technique), and we rallied up
taking out clean highs. Now it's uncertain as to where it wants to go, so the move is done for Aussie Dollar,
and we would have to wait for a new setup. The uncertainty and consolidation nature of this pair would
keep me on the sidelines; I wouldn't worry about trading this one.
New Zealand Dollar (NZD/USD): This is very similar; we would be looking for similar conditions like with the
Aussie Dollar, but for this pair, it looks even worse because we've had an attempt to go lower, lower, lower,
lower every low has been taken out and we don't have any kind of clear defined market structure here
to work with. So, at best, we'd probably see it return back to this low here, and what happens after that is
anybody's guess. So, there's no real clear market structure here to frame analysis on, whereas when we
saw with the Dollar Index and on the Euro, there's potential there. So, there's clarity in Euro Dollar wanting
to potentially go lower and Dollar going higher. Every other pair we've looked at so far is uncertain.

- BULLISH OUTLOOK
Dollar CAD (USD/CAD): We've had an attempt to trade lower below this low progressively, and then it
came back roaring higher, eventually taking out this swing high. It had a retracement, a nice optimal trade
entry long in here, and we also have a consolidation. So, it looks like we could be in for another leg higher
on Dollar CAD, if not for anything else, a run on these double tops. So, a double top sweep we would look
for that to occur. We've already had double bottoms swept here and then rejection, so we would be
anticipating a movement higher to clear out the liquidity above these equal highs and maybe make an
attempt to go a little bit higher up from the range that's created from this high to this low.
What I'm looking for is evidence of a clear direction where it wants to go. When we have areas where we
just recently had these equal lows taken out, like here where we rejected, and we just recently had these
equal lows taken out with this run here and it rejected, and it's also left these equal highs in place. So,
where is the most likely direction going to be? Up. And it's also in favor with what I found in the Dollar Index
at the beginning of the video, so we could probably see a run higher. So, there are two conditions that we
were looking for: Euro Dollar lower, Dollar CAD higher, and Dollar Index higher. Now again, this is the
condition that I would see right now, but the probabilities are not as high because we're so close to the
holidays and it's the end of the year, end of the month. But I'm giving you how I would personally go
through the charts and study and look for opportunities for price movement, just to study how it unfolds on
the daily chart.

- POTENTIAL UPSIDE
Dollar Swissy (USD/CHF): Kind of like what we've seen with the Aussie Dollar, it doesn't have a real
directional bias yet. We do have price coming down below these equal lows; we ran through that and
rejected. So, we may have an attempt to get back above here. But if you go a little bit to the left further, we
have relative equal highs here. So, if we have that Dollar bullishness, you could see another leg higher to
reach above these equal highs. I don't like that one so much, but if we want to talk about probable levels to
reach for Dollar Swissy, we could possibly see it trade up to that level.

- TRADING RANGES AND KEY
LEVELS
Finally, the Dollar Yen (USD/JPY). This pattern is a really obvious trading range. This pair has had a real
hard time finding its way out of this range. I will add on the levels here to save time.
What we're looking at here: I have the double bottoms and old highs noted, and also have the yearly high
defined with this range here, looking back 12 months. We have it defined at the end of this month here,
which would be 12 full months. I also have the ranges with the quarterly, so here's March, and it takes us to
June, September, and we're in December now. Obviously, you can go through and add the individual
vertical lines delineating the individual monthly highs and lows. I don't want to add all that because it would
make the chart really busy. The question is just, 'How many levels would you have?' You're only going to
worry about the ones that are right now.
Right now, where we're trading out here, the range is defined by where we just recently came off this low
and we just recently left this high. So, we're inside that range here. We're a little bit less than halfway below
that. So, if we're expecting bullishness on the dollar, we would reasonably expect the Dollar Yen to make an
attempt to get higher. The problem with this pair is it's really stuck in a range, so you don't want to trade it in
the middle of the range. You want to trade it when it gets down to an old low.

section_title: PRACTICAL APPLICATION OF HIGHER TIME FRAME TRADING ON USD/JPY
content:
You want to anticipate it running an old low, like it did these equal lows here, run that and then we rally. We
have equal lows here; we'd like to see it trade down, maybe take those out, and then rally up, or just leave it
where it's at and start running higher. We had equal highs here, it punched through it here, and then traded
a little lower. We had equal lows here (1, 2, 3, 4), ran through it and also reached below this low here. So,
what we're looking for is old lows to be run out, which it does here, and then rejection. What makes the
rejection? It trades back above that old low. Once that happens, you would look for an old high or equal
highs to reach for. Here's an old high, it goes, trades to that. We have an old high here, it doesn't get to
that, it fails here, trades back down, it goes back below this old high. What's it going to reach for? Possibly
this old low here.
It trades back up to this level here as a key level. This is the reason why it makes these levels key, by
having defined ranges that are generic. They're not dynamic, they're not ambiguous; they are specific:
- Previous weeks' highs and lows
- Monthly highs and lows
- Quarterly highs and lows
- Yearly highs and lows
By focusing only on those and trading when it gets to those levels and nothing else, we're not trading in
here because on a daily timeframe, you're stuck. You have to wait for longer-term setups. So, you're going
to have to wait for these levels to trade back to this high, trade through it, maybe reject it. You'll come home
from work and see that it's done that. So, what do you do in Asia? You can go short, or you can look at the
open at Asia and maybe trade above the opening at Asia and then sell short there with a stop above that
high. It may be a lot of pips because of the daily range. But again, this is for people that cannot day trade.
What you're going to be looking for: we have this low down here, we can hold for that, and it takes a long
time, many, many weeks go by before it ends up blowing out that low here. So, you could have made profit
between this price point here and here. Same scenario when we see this low here, price trades down
below it. We wait for it to do what? Come back above it. It does it here, so we could be a buyer on this day
here and look for what? Price to trade to where? This old high. Why? Because we have equal highs, so
price trades above it, and there's your opportunity right there. This becomes another trade because we
have equal highs. It's rallied through that. What can you be reaching for? Market below these equal lows.
So, it could be a seller on the long position and go short, looking for a run below these equal lows here,
and it does that here. We take your profits, and because we've taken equal lows, we can anticipate a rally
again. Since we had this high take out these equal highs here, we've never came back to this high yet, so
that would be the next level of upside I would expect it to be traded to, and if we get momentum through
that, you can see these double tops taken out.

- FOCUS ON OBVIOUS LIQUIDITY POOLS
That's pretty much what I teach new traders to start with if they're stuck only on this timeframe. It takes a lot
of time for the setups to form; they cannot do anything else until it trades to these types of levels. Why?
Because these are the highest probability levels. Yes, you can do day trading entries, but this whole
teaching was focused primarily on individuals who cannot be on short-term timeframes, they don't have a
lot of time, and they have to be looking for something only on a daily chart. This is how you would do it.
There are a lot of things we could add to this, but I don't want to do too much of that because if you're on
this timeframe, you're probably very new in your development, and I don't want to give you a lot of things to
worry with. Just start looking for:
- Double tops
- Old highs
- Old lows
- Double bottoms
Draw circles on that. Make a notation on your chart. These highs here (yes, we went above a little bit and
rejected, but these are relatively equal highs), so price could come back through that again, and it does.
But notice it leaves this high intact, so this is an untapped area of liquidity. You're probably saying, 'Who
has a stop up there?' Large funds. That's what the market reaches for; it's not your retail stop. Your retail
stop is going to be placed where the books tell you, and it's going to be the wrong place. But the market
goes to large liquidity pools on the basis of other institutional traders or large funds. The market is all
designed to go after that. Your retail stop, they can't see it, they don't know where it's at, it's not important to
them because you're such a small little blip on the radar in terms of liquidity they can't pay anything out with
you. So, large funds, they're trading billions of dollars many times, and that will have a large area of interest
for buying and selling, and the market will gravitate to those levels. But they're going to be relative to these
highs, these lows, and these double tops and double bottoms. You can see how the market goes right to
them and then starts to look for the other side of the marketplace.
So, from a higher time standpoint, basically all you're going to be looking for are obvious double tops and
double bottoms, and single highs and single lows, and where the market most recently came from. Just find
the opposite direction where the liquidity would be below the lows. If it wasn't a double bottom or equal lows
like this, say this was not a low, this would be sufficient because that's where sell stops would be. After
running buy stops above here, the market's going to go lower seeking what? Liquidity. Once it takes that
liquidity out below that low, it's going to do what? Either going to keep going lower for a lower level of
liquidity (which it shouldn't because we've already had many attempts to go here, here, here, here, and
then we're out of the way). So if we go below here, we want to see it trade higher. What's it going to reach
for? We have this short-term high it reached above, and then we have this high right in here. So there's
liquidity above that one, and we certainly have liquidity above there, and ultimately if we get momentum
above that, we could see these stops right there. And that would be in agreement with bullish dollar as well
because the first currency in this pair is the dollar against the yen, so dollar strength in the index would
promote strength in this pair.
